solutions.solution: (d) 2 and 5 form a pair of identical figures.
(c) 3 and 5 form a pair of identical figures.
(c) 2 and 4 form a pair of identical figures.
(c) 3 and 7 form a pair of identical figures.
(c) 1, 3 and 8 form a group of figures which contain one geometrical shape.
(c) 2, 5 and 8 form a group of figures which are composed of two dissimilar figures, one inside the other.
(d) 1, 4), (2, 5) and (5, 6) are a group of three figures.
(b) (2, 6, 8), (1, 5, 9) and (3, 4, 7) are a group of similar shape figures.
(b) (1, 4), (2, 7), (3, 5) and (6, 8) are group of identical figures.
(d) 1 and 3 contain a V-shaped figure inside another figure. 2, 4 and 5 contain one figure placed inside a similar figure. 6 and 7 are divided into four equal parts by mutually perpendicular lines.

(b) 1, 3 and 8 form a group of figures which contain one geometrical shape. 2, 4 and 6 form a group of figures in which one figure is enclosed inside another figure. 5, 7 and 9 form a group of figures in which two figures are attached with one another.
(d) 1, 5 and 9 consist of cup-shaped figures. 3, 6 and 7 contain straight lines dividing the figure into four parts. 2, 4 and 8 are simple geometrical figures.
(b) 1, 4 and 6 contain two identical figures which intersect each other. 2, 5 and 8 contain figures in which curves are present. 3, 7 and 9 contain figures which represent circular design patterns.
(c) 1, 3 and 5 are figures having partially or completely curved boundaries. 2, 6 and 8 are triangles. 4, 7 and 9 are quadrilaterals.
